title: Product BOM Decomposition — General Medical and Surgical Hospitals

sections.title: NAICS Routing Check
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Deployment market NAICS | 622110 — General Medical and Surgical Hospitals (Service sector 62) |
| Manufacturer NAICS (GfS / Assa Abloy) | ~332510 Hardware Manufacturing / 334290 Other Communications Equipment Manufacturing |
| BOM applies? | **YES** — the BOM subject is the Türwächter (manufactured physical hardware), not the hospital's output (patient outcomes). Hospital = deployment context only. |
| Output type (L5) | Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT |

> **Note:** Per routing rules a BOM is not constructed for the hospital's output. A hospital produces patient outcomes, not physical products. This BOM decomposes the Türwächter as it is configured for hospital deployment.

---

sections.title: B.1 — L4 Subsystems
sections.content:
| L4 | Category | Function |
|---|---|---|
| L4.1 Mechanical guard subsystem | Primary functional | Blocks door handle; triggers alarm on 45° swing; key override bypass |
| L4.2 Acoustic alarm subsystem | Primary functional | Generates ≤95 dB alarm on activation; pre-alarm warning tone on touch |
| L4.3 IoT communication subsystem | Control / interface | Transmits guard state and alarm events wirelessly to receiver / BMS gateway |
| L4.4 Power subsystem | Auxiliary | Supplies 9 V to IoT module; acoustic alarm is mechanically independent of power |
| L4.5 Housing / enclosure subsystem | Containment | Surface-mounted casing; mounting base; RAL 6029 green safety identification |

Augmentation check: primary functional core ✓ · control system ✓ · power system ✓ · containment ✓ · key override ✓

---

sections.title: B.2 + B.3 — Decomposition and Alternatives
sections.content:
### L4.1 — Mechanical Guard Subsystem

```
L4  Mechanical guard subsystem
│
├── L3  Guard arm & pivot module
│   ├── L2  Pivot assembly
│   │   └── L1  Pivot pin ──────────── L0  Stainless steel (304/316)
│   └── L2  Return spring assembly
│       └── L1  Torsion spring ──────── L0  Spring steel (65Mn or CrSi alloy)
│
├── L3  Key override module (profile half-cylinder)
│   └── L2  Lock cylinder assembly
│       ├── L1  Cylinder body ─────── L0  Zinc alloy (Zamak 3/5)
│       └── L1  Tumbler pins ──────── L0  Brass (CuZn37)
│
└── L3  Handle block element
    └── L1  Block cam / stop arm ───── L0  Polyamide 66 (PA66) or POM (Acetal)
```

**Alternatives at L3 — Guard mechanism principle**

| Alternative | Share (est.) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Spring-loaded mechanical guard arm (GfS-type) | ~55% | Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate |
| Integrated alarmed push bar (GfS e-Bar, Detex-type) | ~25% | Growing | LOW | Analyst estimate; Detex, commercialdoorhardwaresupply.com (2024) |
| Electromagnetic delayed-egress lock (15-sec) | ~15% | Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate; silvaconsultants.com (2024) |
| Other mechanical restraint configurations | ~5% | Declining | LOW | Analyst estimate |

> **Hospital note:** Electromagnetic delayed-egress is common in psychiatric and dementia wards (NFPA 101 §7.2.1.6 permits 15-second delay). Mechanical guard arm preferred for general corridors, OR exits, and stairwells where instant egress is mandatory.

**Alternatives at L0 — Handle block material**

| Alternative | Share (est.) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Polyamide 66 (PA66) | ~45% | Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate |
| POM (Acetal / Delrin) | ~35% | Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate |
| Die-cast zinc (Zamak) | ~15% | Declining | LOW | Analyst estimate |
| Other engineering plastics | ~5% | Growing | LOW | Analyst estimate |

---

### L4.2 — Acoustic Alarm Subsystem

```
L4  Acoustic alarm subsystem
│
├── L3  Alarm transducer module
│   ├── L2  Piezoelectric transducer assembly
│   │   ├── L1  PZT ceramic disc ────── L0  Lead zirconate titanate (PZT-5A/5H)
│   │   └── L1  Brass diaphragm ──────── L0  Brass (CuZn30)
│   └── L1  Volume adjustment potentiometer ── L0  Carbon resistive film
│
└── L3  Horn / resonating chamber
    └── L1  Horn body ──────────────── L0  ABS thermoplastic
```

**Alternatives at L3 — Alarm transducer type**

| Alternative | Share (est.) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Piezoelectric (PZT) buzzer/horn | ~75% | Stable | MEDIUM | Piezoelectric Buzzer Market (OpenPR, 2024); Business Research Insights, Buzzer Market $722.6M (2024) |
| Electromagnetic moving-armature buzzer | ~20% | Declining | MEDIUM | Cognitive Market Research — electromagnetic buzzers $1,351.5M global (2024); simpler but lower max SPL |
| Electrodynamic mini-speaker | ~5% | Growing | LOW | Analyst estimate; emerging in voice-capable alarm systems |

> **Hospital note:** 95 dB at 1 m violates WHO daytime noise guidelines (40 dB) for patient wards and ICUs. Hospital deployments require software-configurable SPL (65–80 dB for patient-proximate doors). The GfS product supports volume adjustment — verify per variant before specifying.

**Alternatives at L0 — PZT disc material**

| Alternative | Share (est.) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lead zirconate titanate (PZT-5A/5H) | ~80% | Stable | MEDIUM | Industry standard for high-SPL applications |
| Lead-free piezo ceramics (BNT-BT, KNN) | ~15% | Growing | LOW | RoHS pressure; EU Directive 2011/65/EU restricts lead in EEE |
| PVDF polymer piezo film | ~5% | Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate; niche flexible/low-profile configurations |

---

### L4.3 — IoT Communication Subsystem

```
L4  IoT communication subsystem
│
├── L3  RF transceiver module
│   ├── L2  PCB assembly
│   │   ├── L1  Transceiver SoC (868 MHz) ─── L0  Silicon (semiconductor grade)
│   │   ├── L1  MCU ──────────────────────── L0  Silicon
│   │   ├── L1  PCB ──────────────────────── L0  FR4 (glass-reinforced epoxy)
│   │   └── L1  Passive components ─────────── L0  MLCC ceramic (C0G / X5R)
│   └── L1  Antenna (PCB trace or whip) ─────── L0  Copper (Cu)
│
└── L3  State detection module
    ├── L1  Reed switch ──────────────────── L0  Hermetic glass capsule (reed)
    └── L1  Hall-effect sensor (alternative)  ── L0  Silicon
```

**Alternatives at L3 — Wireless protocol (primary design choice)**

| Alternative | Share in building security IoT (est.) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Zigbee 2.4 GHz | ~30% | Stable | MEDIUM | IHS Markit; Emergen Research (2024) — 1.4B active Zigbee devices; dominant in building automation |
| BLE 5.x (Bluetooth Low Energy) | ~25% | Growing | MEDIUM | Emergen Research (2024) — BLE in 82% of smartwatches; strong growth in commercial building security |
| LoRaWAN 868/915 MHz | ~20% | Growing fast | MEDIUM | GM Insights: LoRaWAN market $3.7B (2024), CAGR 41.1% (2025–2034); wall-penetrating range advantage |
| Z-Wave 868 MHz | ~10% | Stable | MEDIUM | IHS Markit; strong residential; less dominant in commercial |
| EasyWave 868 MHz (proprietary GfS) | ~10% | Stable (niche) | LOW | Analyst estimate — proprietary ecosystem, GfS-specific receivers only |
| Wi-Fi 802.11 | ~5% | Declining for battery | MEDIUM | TMR (2024) — dominant overall IoT share but ~10× higher power draw, impractical for 9V battery |

> **Hospital note:** Hospitals standardize on BACnet/IP or KNX via Siemens Desigo CC or Honeywell EBI. EasyWave is not natively interoperable — a GfS BUS gateway (sold separately) is required. LoRaWAN is attractive for large multi-wing campuses due to 868 MHz penetration through reinforced concrete. Zigbee/BLE suit ward-level deployments where gateway infrastructure already exists.

---

### L4.4 — Power Subsystem

```
L4  Power subsystem
│
└── L3  Battery holder module
    ├── L2  Battery contact assembly
    │   ├── L1  Spring contacts ──────── L0  Phosphor bronze (Cu-Sn-P alloy)
    │   └── L1  Contact housing ─────── L0  ABS thermoplastic
    └── L1  Battery holder frame ──────── L0  ABS thermoplastic
    (Battery = consumable, not part of product BOM; chemistry IS a design-choice input)
```

**Alternatives at L0 — Battery chemistry (9 V form factor)**

| Alternative | Share in commercial alarm devices (est.) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 9V alkaline (Zn-MnO2) | ~60% | Declining | MEDIUM | Industry standard for commercial alarms; GP Batteries, Duracell, Energizer |
| 9V lithium primary (Li-FeS2) | ~30% | Growing | MEDIUM | 3× longer shelf life; better at extreme temps; preferred for infrequently accessed doors |
| 9V NiMH rechargeable | ~10% | Stable (niche) | LOW | Analyst estimate; requires recharge infrastructure |

> **Hospital note:** Hospital biomedical engineering departments often mandate specific battery brands for maintenance inventory control. Lithium 9V (e.g., Energizer Ultimate Lithium L522) is preferred for ward doors with high activation frequency — reduces maintenance callouts and battery-failure alarm gaps (Constraint C4).

---

### L4.5 — Housing / Enclosure Subsystem

```
L4  Housing / enclosure subsystem
│
├── L3  Guard arm housing
│   └── L2  Guard arm body (RAL 6029 safety green)
│       └── L1  Molded guard body ──────── L0  ABS thermoplastic
│
├── L3  Mounting base
│   ├── L2  Base plate assembly
│   │   ├── L1  Base plate ─────────────── L0  ABS or Polycarbonate (PC)
│   │   └── L1  Mounting fasteners ─────── L0  Stainless steel A2-70
│   └── L1  Glass-frame adapter (Art. 991170, optional) ── L0  ABS
│
└── L3  Identification / labelling element
    └── L1  Warning label ──────────────── L0  Polycarbonate film (UV-stable)
```

**Alternatives at L0 — Housing material**

| Alternative | Share in safety device housings (est.) | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ABS thermoplastic | ~60% | Stable | MEDIUM | Dominant in commercial door hardware; cost-effective; Grand View Research (2024) |
| Polycarbonate (PC) | ~20% | Growing | MEDIUM | PC electrical enclosures market $489M (2024); higher impact/temperature resistance |
| PC/ABS blend | ~15% | Stable | MEDIUM | Business Research Insights PC/ABS $4.94B (2024); balance of both properties |
| Glass-filled polyamide (PA-GF) | ~5% | Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate; niche industrial/high-temp |

> **Hospital note:** Antimicrobial ABS (silver-ion or copper-infused) is an emerging requirement for door hardware in clinical zones (ICU, OR ante-room, neonatal unit). No GfS antimicrobial housing variant is currently listed — this is a **product gap** vs. dormakaba SafeRoute and ASSA ABLOY's own CLIQ range, which offer antimicrobial finish options.

---

sections.title: Hospital-Specific Adaptation Matrix
sections.content:
| BOM Position | Adaptation Required | Severity | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| L4.2 → L3 transducer | Configurable alarm volume (95 dB → 65–80 dB) for patient-proximate doors | HIGH | Confirm volume-adjustable firmware available per variant before specifying |
| L4.3 → L3 protocol | BACnet/IP or KNX gateway for Siemens Desigo / Honeywell EBI | HIGH | GfS BUS gateway accessory required; add to project BOM |
| L4.5 → L0 housing | Antimicrobial surface treatment for clinical zone installations | MEDIUM | Product gap — no current GfS SKU; workaround: antimicrobial cover/trim sourced separately |
| L4.4 → L0 battery | Lithium 9V for high-traffic ward doors; biomedical standardization | MEDIUM | Specify Energizer L522 or equivalent in hospital installation spec |
| L4.1 → L3 mechanism | No structural change needed; EN 179/1125 hardware fits standard hospital fire doors | NONE | — |

---

bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Key override module
bom_units.description: Profile half-cylinder lock for authorized bypass without triggering alarm
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Handle block element
bom_units.description: Cam or stop-arm that prevents door handle depression in locked state
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Acoustic alarm subsystem
bom_units.description: Piezoelectric alarm horn (≤95 dB) triggered mechanically; independent of electrical power
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Alarm transducer module
bom_units.description: PZT-based buzzer/horn assembly with volume adjustment; delivers ≤95 dB SPL
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Horn / resonating chamber
bom_units.description: ABS horn body that shapes and amplifies the transducer output
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: IoT communication subsystem
bom_units.description: 868 MHz EasyWave radio module that transmits guard-state and alarm events to centralized receivers
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: RF transceiver module
bom_units.description: SoC + antenna + PCB assembly implementing 868 MHz radio protocol
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: State detection module
bom_units.description: Reed switch or Hall-effect sensor detecting guard open/closed state
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Power subsystem
bom_units.description: 9V battery holder supplying IoT module; mechanical alarm operates without battery power
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Battery holder module
bom_units.description: Spring-contact holder for 9V block battery; tool-free battery replacement
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Housing / enclosure subsystem
bom_units.description: Surface-mounted ABS casing in RAL 6029 safety green; mounting base; warning labels
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Guard arm housing
bom_units.description: Outer casing of guard arm in RAL 6029 green ABS
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_units.name: Mounting base
bom_units.description: Base plate and fastener set for door-handle surface mount; includes glass-frame adapter option
bom_units.output_type: Emergency Exit Security Device with IoT

bom_alternatives.name: Piezoelectric (PZT) buzzer/horn
bom_alternatives.source: Piezoelectric Buzzer Market (OpenPR, 2024); Business Research Insights Buzzer Market $722.6M (2024)

bom_alternatives.name: Electromagnetic moving-armature buzzer
bom_alternatives.source: Cognitive Market Research — electromagnetic buzzers $1,351.5M global (2024)

bom_alternatives.name: Zigbee 2.4 GHz
bom_alternatives.source: IHS Markit; Emergen Research IoT Protocol Market (2024) — 1.4B active Zigbee devices

bom_alternatives.name: Wi-Fi 802.11
bom_alternatives.source: TMR IoT Protocol Market (2024) — dominant overall but power-impractical for 9V battery

bom_alternatives.name: 9V alkaline (Zn-MnO2)
bom_alternatives.source: Industry standard; GP Batteries, Duracell, Energizer

bom_alternatives.name: 9V lithium primary (Li-FeS2)
bom_alternatives.source: Analyst estimate; 3× shelf life advantage documented by Energizer product data

bom_alternatives.name: ABS thermoplastic
bom_alternatives.source: Grand View Research polycarbonate/plastic enclosures market (2024)
